From mass threshold to molecular biology

A nine-phase migration of the v2ecoli chromosome-replication-initiation model. Each phase replaces one piece of the baseline heuristic with the molecular biology it stands in for, validates the change with a before/after sim, and stacks on the previous phases.

Architecture: replication_initiation  |  Phase progress: 9/9 done  |  Snapshots in the full-architecture sim: 43  |  Curated reference: PDF · Markdown summary

What this architecture does, and why

v2ecoli is a single-cell whole-cell simulator of E. coli — every protein, RNA, metabolic flux, and genome position represented and updated each timestep. Replication of the 4.6 Mb chromosome is one of those processes: a step in the simulation decides when a new round of replication fires from the single origin, oriC, and how the two replisomes that result march outward to the terminus.

The baseline chromosome-replication step decides this using a single heuristic — cell mass per oriC ≥ a critical mass — calibrated so the average cell-cycle timing comes out right. The threshold itself is a number ("ParCa-fit", from Parameter Calculator, the offline pipeline that fits hundreds of constants from the published E. coli data so the simulation reproduces measured growth rates and abundances). DnaA appears in the baseline only as a translated protein with a fixed copy-number budget — not as the regulatory driver it actually is in the cell.

In vivo, the cell decides when to initiate replication through a network of DnaA-centered interactions: DnaA loads ATP and binds boxes near oriC; once enough are loaded, the origin opens and fires; the moving replisome then hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP to keep it from firing again; helper sequences elsewhere on the chromosome re-activate inactive DnaA later in the cycle; SeqA and DnaA-promoter feedback set the timing. The replication_initiation architecture replaces the mass-per-oriC heuristic with that network, in nine cumulative phases that each build on the previous.

Glossary — the players and what they do

DnaA
The master initiator protein. Cycles between three nucleotide states: apo (no nucleotide), DnaA-ATP (active), DnaA-ADP (inactive). Only DnaA-ATP can fully drive origin opening.
oriC
The single chromosomal origin of replication, ~462 bp, containing 11 DnaA boxes — short DNA sequences that bind DnaA. Three (R1, R2, R4) are high-affinity (Kd ≈ 1 nM, bind both ATP- and ADP-DnaA); the other 8 are low-affinity (Kd > 100 nM, ATP-form only, fill cooperatively). The cooperative low-affinity load is what makes initiation a switch.
DOR / DUE
Two functional regions of oriC: DOR (DnaA-Oligomerization Region) is the DnaA-box platform on which DnaA assembles into a filament; DUE (Duplex Unwinding Element) is the AT-rich segment that the DnaA filament locally unwinds, exposing single-stranded DNA for the helicase.
RIDA
Regulatory Inactivation of DnaA. The β-clamp of the moving replisome (DnaN) recruits an ADP-loaded version of the protein Hda; the clamp+Hda complex catalyzes hydrolysis of DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADP. RIDA is the dominant DnaA-inactivation pathway whenever a replisome is active, and is what prevents re-initiation within the same cell cycle.
DDAH
datA-Dependent ATP Hydrolysis. A backup hydrolysis pathway that operates at a separate locus, datA (94.7 min on the E. coli chromosome), where IHF binding induces a DNA loop that catalytically hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP. Less powerful than RIDA, but constitutive — runs whether or not replication is active.
DARS1 / DARS2
DnaA-Reactivating Sequences. Two non-coding loci that catalyze release of ADP from DnaA-ADP, returning DnaA to its apo form (which then re-loads ATP from the cytoplasmic pool). DARS2 is dominant in vivo and is gated cell-cycle timing by IHF and Fis binding. Together with RIDA, DARS closes the DnaA nucleotide-state loop.
SeqA
A protein that binds hemimethylated GATC sequences — sites where one strand is Dam-methylated and the other isn't, which is the state of newly-replicated DNA before Dam catches up. oriC is unusually GATC-rich, so right after firing it gets coated by SeqA for ~10 min, blocking DnaA re-binding and preventing premature re-initiation.
IHF / Fis
Two nucleoid-associated proteins that bend DNA. Both act as structural cofactors at oriC, datA, and DARS2 — their cell-cycle-timed binding gates when those loci can do their DnaA-related job. Already present in v2ecoli but their regulatory role at these specific loci is not yet wired.
dnaA promoter (p1, p2)
The promoter of the dnaA gene itself, with two transcription start sites: p1 (basal level) and p2 (~3× stronger). The 7 DnaA boxes between them turn DnaA into a repressor of its own gene — the negative feedback that closes the loop on the whole cycle.
ParCa
Parameter Calculator. The one-time pre-processing pipeline that, given the published E. coli proteomic / transcriptomic / metabolomic data, derives the per-gene transcription rates, per-protein degradation rates, biomass coefficients, etc. that the simulation needs. Anything labeled "ParCa-fit" in this report is a number that came from that pipeline rather than from a first-principles formula.

What's already present in the baseline model

  • DnaA the protein. Expressed via the generic transcription / translation pipeline (the same machinery used for every protein in v2ecoli) and accumulates as bulk PD03831[c] (apo-DnaA monomer). Copy number tracks cell mass.
  • Equilibrium nucleotide loading. Two mass-action reactions in the equilibrium step exchange apo-DnaA with cellular ATP and ADP, giving DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP / apo-DnaA species in fast equilibrium with the cytoplasmic nucleotide pools. Without a hydrolysis pathway, DnaA-ATP just sits at its equilibrium ceiling — that's the regulatory-emptiness Phase 1 diagnoses.
  • ~307 candidate DnaA boxes placed on the chromosome by a bioinformatic motif search for the consensus 9-mer TTWTNCACA. Far more than the 30 named in the curated reference, because most are random matches in the genomic background.
  • SeqA as a translated protein (~1029 copies in the initial state), but no DNA-binding activity wired.
  • Mass-threshold initiation step. Fires when cell mass per oriC crosses the ParCa-fit critical value; then advances the resulting replisomes along the chromosome and writes the new replicated DNA into the bulk store.

What's missing, and what each phase adds

The phases are cumulative: each turns on a feature flag in the architecture builder and stacks on top of the previous. The before/after panels under each phase compare two such slices — "before" is the cumulative architecture immediately upstream of the phase; "after" is that same slice plus the new phase. Status pills are auto-detected from the codebase (whether the relevant file exists, whether the splice is wired, whether the listener emits) so they cannot drift from the source.

  1. Phase 0 — DnaA-box region classifier. Bookkeeping. The ~307 consensus DnaA boxes are already in the model as unique-store entries; Phase 0 just adds a coordinate-based classifier region_for_coord that labels each one with its regulatory region (oriC, dnaA-promoter, datA, DARS1, DARS2, or "other" for the genomic background). All 307 then become available to Phase 2 for binding and titration — the named regions are the regulatory drivers, and the ~280 "other" boxes are the titration sink that keeps free DnaA-ATP low.
  2. Phase 1 — Surface the DnaA pools. Adds a listener that emits apo / DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP counts each tick. Lets the report confirm the diagnosis: in the baseline architecture, DnaA-ATP runs at its equilibrium ceiling because nothing hydrolyzes it.
  3. Phase 2 — DnaA-box binding (with titration). Each tick, sample which DnaA boxes are bound from the equilibrium occupancy probability p = [DnaA] / (Kd + [DnaA]) per affinity tier, then allocate the actual cytoplasmic DnaA pool to the samples in priority order (highest affinity first, lowest last). The bound DnaA molecules are subtracted from the cytoplasmic bulk[DnaA-ATP] / bulk[DnaA-ADP] pools for the next tick — so the ~280 background "other" boxes titrate the free pool, lowering the concentration that drives named-region binding. Phase 3 reads bound_oric_low from this listener.
  4. Phase 3 — DnaA-gated initiation. Replaces the mass-per-oriC threshold with a check on oriC occupancy: fire when at least 4 of the 8 cooperative low-affinity DnaA boxes at oriC are loaded with DnaA-ATP (≈ "right-arm filament assembled", per the curated PDF). The gate reads bound_oric_low from the Phase 2 binding listener, so the trigger is the actual molecular configuration rather than a cytoplasmic concentration proxy. Same self-limiting shape as the baseline mass gate — n_oriC doubles after firing, bound_oric_low briefly drops, and SeqA (Phase 4) holds the gate shut while RIDA (Phase 5) clears the DnaA-ATP pool.
  5. Phase 4 — SeqA sequestration window. After each initiation event, force the gate to 0 for ~10 min. Models SeqA binding hemimethylated GATC sites at the newly-replicated origin and physically blocking DnaA from rebinding.
  6. Phase 5 — RIDA hydrolysis flux. A dedicated step that hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADP at a rate proportional to the active replisome count. Models the β-clamp + Hda complex on the moving replisome — the dominant pathway for preventing re-initiation within the same cycle.
  7. Phase 6 — DDAH backup hydrolysis. A constitutive first-order DnaA-ATP hydrolysis term, modeling the IHF-induced loop at datA. Smaller rate than RIDA, but runs whether or not a replisome is active — keeps DnaA-ATP from drifting up between rounds.
  8. Phase 7 — DARS reactivation. A first-order DnaA-ADP → apo-DnaA conversion. Combined with the still-active equilibrium re-loading of apo-DnaA with ATP, this closes the nucleotide cycle (ATP → ADP via RIDA/DDAH; ADP → apo via DARS; apo → ATP via equilibrium) and stabilizes the DnaA-ATP fraction inside the literature 30–70% band.
  9. Phase 8 — dnaA promoter autoregulation. Read the binding listener's count of bound boxes at the dnaA promoter each tick, and scale the dnaA gene's transcription rate down in proportion: rate = baseline × (1 − max_repression × fraction_bound). Negative feedback that closes the loop on the entire cycle.

The four Parts below group these phases by what question they answer: codify what's there → drive the DnaA cycle → bind to the chromosome and gate initiation → add regulatory feedbacks. Phase numbers track implementation order; the table renders them in narrative order under each Part.

#PhaseStatusWhat it does
Part I — Foundations
P0Region classifier — partition the 307 consensus boxesdoneThe bioinformatic strict-consensus search at `flat/sequence_motifs.tsv` finds ~307 distinct DnaA boxes (TTWTNCACA variants) across the chromosome. They are <strong>already in the model</strong> — instantiated as unique-store entries in the <code>DnaA_box</code> array every cell. Phase 0 just adds a coordinate-to-region classifier (<code>region_for_coord(bp)</code>) so later phases can ask "which boxes are at oriC?" instead of "which boxes fall between bp 3,925,629 and 3,926,090?". No schema change — region is derived from each box's existing <code>coordinates</code> field. All 307 are available to Phase 2 for binding; about 30 fall in the five named regulatory regions, and the remaining ~280 are the genomic-background pool that titrates DnaA-ATP.
P1Expose the DnaA nucleotide-state poolsdoneWire a listener that emits apo / DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP pool counts every step. Pool dynamics were already running underneath (the equilibrium reactions for both nucleotide-bound forms have been firing in the cache since the start) but were invisible from the trajectory; Phase 1 surfaces them so the rest of the work can be measured against the literature steady-state band.
Part II — The DnaA nucleotide cycle
P5RIDA — DnaA-ATP hydrolysis at the replisomedoneAdd a dedicated `RIDA` Step that hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP to DnaA-ADP at a rate ∝ active replisome count, and deactivate the DnaA-ADP equilibrium reaction so RIDA's output accumulates instead of being instantly re-dissociated. Hda + Hda-β-clamp complex (CPLX0-10342) and the FBA-level reaction (RXN0-7444) were already in the data; Phase 5 wires the kinetic process and the equilibrium override.
P7DARS reactivation — closes the DnaA cycledoneAdd a dedicated `DARS` Step that releases ADP from DnaA-ADP at a first-order rate, regenerating apo-DnaA. Combined with the still-active `MONOMER0-160_RXN` equilibrium that re-loads apo-DnaA with cellular ATP, this closes the loop opened in Phase 5. Steady-state DnaA-ATP fraction lands inside the literature 30–70% band. Per-locus DARS1 vs DARS2 and IHF/Fis modulation are deferred follow-ups.
Part III — From cytoplasm to chromosome
P2DnaA-box binding + chromosomal titrationdoneAdd a DnaABoxBinding step that samples per-box equilibrium occupancy each tick from the DnaA-ATP/ADP bulk pools and per-region affinity classes. Emits per-region bound counts (including the ~99 background boxes in `other`, which sequester most of the DnaA pool — the chromosomal titration buffer described in the curated reference). Currently a listener: bound counts are reported but not yet subtracted from the free pool.
P3Replace mass-threshold initiation gatedoneSubstitute `DnaAGatedChromosomeReplication` for the baseline `ChromosomeReplication`. The new gate fires when at least 4 of the 8 cooperative low-affinity DnaA boxes at oriC are loaded with DnaA-ATP (≈ "right-arm filament assembled" per the curated PDF: R4 → C1 → I3 → C2 → C3). The gate reads `bound_oric_low` from the Phase 2 binding listener, so the trigger is the actual molecular configuration on the chromosome, not a cytoplasmic concentration proxy. Per-tick state-merge ordering means the gate sees `bound_oric_low = 0` at t=0 (before the binding step has sampled), so the cell starts in the cache's correct state of 1 chromosome / 2 oriC / 2 forks rather than firing an extra initiation in tick 0.
Part IV — Regulatory feedbacks
P4SeqA sequestration window after initiationdoneSeqA the protein (`EG12197-MONOMER`) is already expressed (~1029 copies in the init state); Phase 4 wires its *activity*. The DnaA-gated step records the time of each initiation event and forces its gate ratio to 0 for the next ~10 minutes — modeling SeqA binding to hemimethylated GATC sites at the newly-replicated origin and blocking DnaA rebinding during the window.
P6DDAH — backup DnaA-ATP hydrolysis at datAdoneAdd a DDAH Step that hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP at a small constitutive rate, modeling the catalytic effect of the datA-IHF complex described in the curated reference. Pairs with Phase 5 (RIDA): RIDA dominates while replisomes are active, DDAH provides a steady background drain that runs even between replication rounds.
P8dnaA promoter autoregulation — close the loop on the DnaA cycledoneAdd a dedicated `DnaAAutoregulation` Step that reads the binding listener's `bound_dnaA_promoter` count each tick and rescales the dnaA TU's `basal_prob` in the live `TranscriptInitiation` instance. The repression factor is `1 - max_repression × bound/total`, identity at zero occupancy and minimum `1 - max_repression` at saturation. The Parca-fit baseline is captured at attach-time and re-applied each tick, so the feedback is fully reversible. p1 vs p2 splitting and the DnaA-ADP boost reported at p2 are deferred follow-ups.

Part I — Foundations

Before changing the biology, take stock. v2ecoli already places ~307 candidate DnaA-binding sites on the chromosome (matches to the consensus 9-mer TTWTNCACA) — they're instantiated as unique-store entries from t=0 — but the baseline architecture treats them as inert. None of them bind anything; none of them gate initiation. Phase 0 adds a coordinate-to-region classifier so later phases can ask "which boxes are at oriC?" instead of "which boxes fall between bp 3,925,629 and 3,926,090?". The classification splits the 307 boxes into ~30 in the five named regulatory regions and ~280 in the genomic-background "other" pool. All 307 are then available to Phase 2 for binding (and titration). Phase 1 adds a listener that emits the apo / DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP pool counts each tick — confirming the diagnosis that in the baseline architecture the DnaA-ATP pool sits at its equilibrium ceiling because nothing hydrolyzes it.

Phase 0 — Region classifier — partition the 307 consensus boxes

done
What this phase does: The bioinformatic strict-consensus search at `flat/sequence_motifs.tsv` finds ~307 distinct DnaA boxes (TTWTNCACA variants) across the chromosome. They are <strong>already in the model</strong> — instantiated as unique-store entries in the <code>DnaA_box</code> array every cell. Phase 0 just adds a coordinate-to-region classifier (<code>region_for_coord(bp)</code>) so later phases can ask "which boxes are at oriC?" instead of "which boxes fall between bp 3,925,629 and 3,926,090?". No schema change — region is derived from each box's existing <code>coordinates</code> field. All 307 are available to Phase 2 for binding; about 30 fall in the five named regulatory regions, and the remaining ~280 are the genomic-background pool that titrates DnaA-ATP.
Auto-check: region_for_coord and REGION_BOUNDARIES in molecular_reference  ·  56 tests

Where the named regions sit on the chromosome

A schematic view of the E. coli chromosome (~4.64 Mb circle) with oriC at 12 o'clock, ter at 6, and each named region marked at its absolute bp position. The arc between any two regions is to scale, so the sparsity of named regions vs. the genomic background (~99% of the 307 strict-consensus boxes) is visible at a glance.

Before — current model behavior

Today the chromosome carries DnaA boxes from a single global motif set, with no per-region breakdown. Below is the expected per-region count when the classifier is applied to the existing init-state coordinates.

RegionExpected DnaA-box count
oriC11
dnaA promoter7
datA4
DARS13
DARS25

After — Phase 0 (current state)

Coverage: 8/30 named boxes are caught by the strict-consensus motif. The remaining 22 are non-consensus low-affinity sites that Phase 2 must enrich into the box list.

Caveats & deferred follow-ups
  • Per-box affinity differentiation within "other" is not tracked. Every background box is treated as low-affinity ATP-preferential (Kd ≈ 100 nM); a more refined model would score each match by its motif-position similarity to the consensus.
  • The strict-consensus search picks out high-affinity boxes. Named low-affinity boxes (R5M, τ2, I1-I3, C1-C3 at oriC; box3-c at the dnaA promoter) are not in <code>motif_coordinates</code> as separate coordinate entries — Phase 2 reconstructs them via the curated PDF affinity profile instead.

Phase 1 — Expose the DnaA nucleotide-state pools

done
What this phase does: Wire a listener that emits apo / DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP pool counts every step. Pool dynamics were already running underneath (the equilibrium reactions for both nucleotide-bound forms have been firing in the cache since the start) but were invisible from the trajectory; Phase 1 surfaces them so the rest of the work can be measured against the literature steady-state band.
Auto-check: listener emits all three pool counts; RIDA (Phase 5) and DARS (Phase 7) are wired, so the DnaA-ATP fraction can reach the literature band  ·  4 tests

Pool drivers — what affects DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP today

ProcessEffect on poolBiological meaning
✓transcription + translation (dnaA gene)∅ → apo-DnaASynthesis of DnaA monomers from the dnaA gene.
✓equilibrium step (MONOMER0-160_RXN)apo-DnaA + ATP ⇌ DnaA-ATPReversible nucleotide binding; mass-action driven.
✓equilibrium step (MONOMER0-4565_RXN)apo-DnaA + ADP ⇌ DnaA-ADPReversible nucleotide binding; mass-action driven.
✓protein degradationDnaA → ∅Routine proteolytic turnover of DnaA monomers.
○RIDA (RXN0-7444 in metabolism)DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADPReplication-coupled hydrolysis of DnaA-ATP, catalyzed by the DNA-loaded β-clamp + Hda complex. Reaction is in flat data and FBA but flux is currently zero.
○DDAH (datA + IHF)DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADPBackup DnaA-ATP hydrolysis at the datA locus, gated by IHF binding. Not yet represented.
○DARS1 / DARS2 reactivationDnaA-ADP → apo-DnaA → DnaA-ATPNucleotide release at the DARS loci (modulated by IHF and Fis at DARS2), regenerating active DnaA-ATP. Not yet represented.
○DnaA-box binding (oriC, dnaA promoter, datA, DARS)DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP ⇌ box-bound DnaASequestration of DnaA on chromosomal binding sites, depleting the cytoplasmic pool. Not yet represented in v2ecoli (DnaA_bound is write-only).

Reference-value provenance

3 of 7 reference values come from outside the curated PDF reference list. Review the flagged rows before relying on them as load-bearing parameters.

QuantityValueSourceCitation + caveat
biological steady-state min0.3in curated PDFHansen FG, Atlung T. The DnaA tale. Front. Microbiol. 9:319 (2018).
Lower edge of the cell-cycle-averaged DnaA-ATP fraction window discussed in the review; specific value is approximate field consensus, re-verify before relying on it as a load-bearing parameter.
biological steady-state max0.7in curated PDFHansen FG, Atlung T. The DnaA tale. Front. Microbiol. 9:319 (2018).
Upper edge of the cell-cycle-averaged window; same caveat as the lower bound.
pre-initiation peak0.85outside curated PDFKurokawa K, Nishida S, Emoto A, Sekimizu K, Katayama T. Replication cycle-coordinated change of the adenine nucleotide-bound forms of DnaA protein in Escherichia coli. EMBO J. 18(23):6642–6652 (1999).
Pre-initiation peak; specific number not verified in the curated reference — Kurokawa et al. 1999 is the frequently-cited primary source but is not in our PDF.
post-RIDA trough0.2outside curated PDFKurokawa K, Nishida S, Emoto A, Sekimizu K, Katayama T. Replication cycle-coordinated change of the adenine nucleotide-bound forms of DnaA protein in Escherichia coli. EMBO J. 18(23):6642–6652 (1999).
Post-initiation trough after RIDA fires; same caveat — primary citation is outside the curated reference.
K_d(ATP) [nM]30in curated PDFSpeck C, Weigel C, Messer W. ATP- and ADP-DnaA protein, a molecular switch in gene regulation. EMBO J. 18(21):6169–6176 (1999).
Order-of-magnitude estimate from Speck 1999. The paper reports both forms binding at low-nM affinity; the specific 30 nM value should be re-verified.
K_d(ADP) [nM]80in curated PDFSpeck C, Weigel C, Messer W. ATP- and ADP-DnaA protein, a molecular switch in gene regulation. EMBO J. 18(21):6169–6176 (1999).
Order-of-magnitude estimate from Speck 1999; ATP form is somewhat tighter than ADP form, exact ratio varies by preparation.
cellular ATP/ADP ratio10outside curated PDFBennett BD, Kimball EH, Gao M, Osterhout R, Van Dien SJ, Rabinowitz JD. Absolute metabolite concentrations and implied enzyme active site occupancy in Escherichia coli. Nat. Chem. Biol. 5(8):593–599 (2009).
Order-of-magnitude cellular ATP/ADP ratio in fast-growing E. coli; primary citation is outside the curated PDF.

Before — baseline (no extras; mass-threshold initiation gate)

Observed final DnaA-ATP fraction 100% sits above the literature band (30%–70%). The DnaA-ATP equilibrium is dominating; RIDA (Phase 5) is the missing kinetic force that hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADP.

After — replication_initiation (gate + SeqA + DDAH)

Observed final DnaA-ATP fraction 2% sits below the band; DARS flux not strong enough to balance RIDA. Tune k_rida / k_dars.

Caveats & deferred follow-ups
  • Phase 2 (DnaA-box binding) is wired as a listener-only process today — bound boxes are counted but not yet subtracted from the free DnaA pool. The observed pool counts represent total DnaA, not free DnaA.
Test coverage (hover items for descriptions)
  • ✓ tests/test_dnaA_nucleotide_pool.py4 tests
    test_init_state_has_apo_dnaA_no_bound_forms — Before equilibrium runs, apo-DnaA holds the entire DnaA pool.
    test_equilibrium_partitions_apo_into_nucleotide_bound_forms — One minute of sim is enough for the equilibrium reaction
    test_listener_emits_dnaA_pool_counts — ``listeners.replication_data`` exposes apo / ATP / ADP pool counts
    test_total_dnaA_pool_size_is_consistent_with_translation — The sum apo + ATP-bound + ADP-bound is the total DnaA monomer count

Part II — The DnaA nucleotide cycle

Replication initiation in E. coli is gated by the active form of DnaA — DnaA-ATP. Rather than producing DnaA-ATP afresh each cell cycle, the cell cycles a constant DnaA pool through three nucleotide states: apo (no nucleotide bound), DnaA-ATP (active), and DnaA-ADP (inactive). The two phases in this Part wire the protein-mediated reactions that move DnaA between those states. Phase 5 (RIDA) is the inactivation arm — the β-clamp on the moving replisome pairs with Hda to hydrolyze DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADP. Phase 7 (DARS) is the reactivation arm — the DARS1/DARS2 chromosomal loci release ADP from DnaA-ADP, returning DnaA to apo (which then re-loads ATP via the still-active equilibrium reaction). With both arms wired, the DnaA-ATP fraction reaches a steady state inside the 30–70% band that the literature observes; without DARS, RIDA monotonically depletes DnaA-ATP.

Phase 5 — RIDA — DnaA-ATP hydrolysis at the replisome

done
What this phase does: Add a dedicated `RIDA` Step that hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP to DnaA-ADP at a rate ∝ active replisome count, and deactivate the DnaA-ADP equilibrium reaction so RIDA's output accumulates instead of being instantly re-dissociated. Hda + Hda-β-clamp complex (CPLX0-10342) and the FBA-level reaction (RXN0-7444) were already in the data; Phase 5 wires the kinetic process and the equilibrium override.
Auto-check: rida.RIDA process wired into the architecture; MONOMER0-4565_RXN equilibrium deactivated so RIDA flux accumulates DnaA-ADP  ·  5 tests

Architecture changes — what this phase adds, modifies, overrides

KindSummaryFileDetail / wiring
New processRIDA Stepv2ecoli/processes/rida.pyA dedicated EcoliStep that hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADP each tick. Rate = k · n_active_replisomes · [DnaA-ATP] · dt, drawn from a Poisson distribution.
reads: bulk[MONOMER0-160[c]], bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], unique.active_replisome._entryState, timestep
writes: bulk[MONOMER0-160[c]], bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], listeners.rida.flux_atp_to_adp, listeners.rida.active_replisomes, listeners.rida.rate_constant
OverrideDeactivate MONOMER0-4565_RXN equilibriumv2ecoli/generate_replication_initiation.pyAfter the baseline document is built, the live `ecoli-equilibrium` step instance has its `stoichMatrix` column for `MONOMER0-4565_RXN` zeroed. The equilibrium SS solver still computes a flux for this reaction but the result is multiplied by an all-zero stoichiometry vector, so molecule counts are not coupled by mass-action.
reads: configs.ecoli-equilibrium.stoichMatrix
writes: ecoli-equilibrium.instance.stoichMatrix, ecoli-equilibrium.instance._deactivated_reactions
ListenerNew rida.* listener fieldsv2ecoli/processes/rida.pyEach tick the RIDA Step emits its hydrolysis flux, the active replisome count it observed, and the configured rate constant.
writes: listeners.rida.flux_atp_to_adp, listeners.rida.active_replisomes, listeners.rida.rate_constant
DataBulk-ID constants for DnaA formsv2ecoli/data/replication_initiation/molecular_reference.py`DNAA_APO_BULK_ID = "PD03831[c]"`, `DNAA_ATP_BULK_ID = "MONOMER0-160[c]"`, `DNAA_ADP_BULK_ID = "MONOMER0-4565[c]"`. Imported by RIDA and the replication_data listener.

Process connections (live bigraph subset)

Is RIDA a constraint on metabolism? No. The FBA reaction RXN0-7444 exists in flat/metabolic_reactions.tsv and is registered with metabolism (stoichiometry, catalyst, base reaction) but it carries no kinetic constraint and no biomass demand, so FBA pushes zero flux through it. The dedicated RIDA Step runs in parallel to metabolism: both Steps read and write the shared bulk store directly, but only RIDA produces the observable DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADP flux today. The bigraph below is the live wiring extracted from the composite.

Rendered by bigraph_viz.plot_bigraph from the live replication_initiation composite. Three Steps shown: the new rida, plus ecoli-equilibrium and ecoli-metabolism (which read/write the same DnaA bulk pools).

Chromosome dynamics — replisomes drive the RIDA flux

RIDA's rate is gated on the active-replisome count. The disc at each snapshot shows how many forks are currently engaged with the chromosome — that's the substrate pool RIDA reads each tick.

Before — baseline (no extras; mass-threshold initiation gate)

After — + RIDA (no DARS, no binding; mass gate)

Without DARS (Phase 7) the cycle is open-loop: RIDA monotonically depletes DnaA-ATP, ending the run at 4%. The dip into the literature band is real but transient; Phase 7 closes the cycle by regenerating DnaA-ATP from DnaA-ADP via DARS1/2.

Caveats & deferred follow-ups
  • Without DARS (Phase 7), the cycle is open-loop: DnaA-ATP monotonically depletes during a sim. The dip into the literature band is transient.
  • The rate constant `rate_per_replisome_per_s = 0.005` is a first-pass tuning parameter; should be re-verified against measured DnaA-ATP cell-cycle dynamics.
Test coverage (hover items for descriptions)
  • ✓ tests/test_rida.py5 tests
    test_rida_step_in_cell_state — rida step in cell state
    test_dnaA_adp_equilibrium_deactivated — Without deactivation, mass-action against cellular ADP would
    test_rida_lowers_atp_fraction_into_literature_band — After ~60s of sim, the DnaA-ATP fraction drops from its
    test_rida_flux_listener_emits — The rida listener exposes flux_atp_to_adp and active_replisomes
    test_dnaA_adp_count_grows_with_replication — DnaA-ADP rises monotonically while replisomes are active.

Phase 7 — DARS reactivation — closes the DnaA cycle

done
What this phase does: Add a dedicated `DARS` Step that releases ADP from DnaA-ADP at a first-order rate, regenerating apo-DnaA. Combined with the still-active `MONOMER0-160_RXN` equilibrium that re-loads apo-DnaA with cellular ATP, this closes the loop opened in Phase 5. Steady-state DnaA-ATP fraction lands inside the literature 30–70% band. Per-locus DARS1 vs DARS2 and IHF/Fis modulation are deferred follow-ups.
Auto-check: dars.DARS process wired into the architecture; closes the DnaA-ATP/ADP cycle with RIDA (Phase 5)  ·  4 tests

Architecture changes — what this phase adds, modifies, overrides

KindSummaryFileDetail / wiring
New processDARS Stepv2ecoli/processes/dars.pyFirst-order release of ADP from DnaA-ADP, regenerating apo-DnaA. Rate = k_dars · [DnaA-ADP] · dt, Poisson-drawn. The freed apo-DnaA is then reloaded with ATP by the still-active MONOMER0-160_RXN equilibrium, completing the cycle.
reads: bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], bulk[PD03831[c]], timestep
writes: bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], bulk[PD03831[c]], listeners.dars.flux_adp_to_apo, listeners.dars.rate_constant
ListenerNew dars.* listener fieldsv2ecoli/processes/dars.pyPer-tick DARS reactivation flux + configured rate constant.
writes: listeners.dars.flux_adp_to_apo, listeners.dars.rate_constant

How DARS closes the cycle

How DARS closes the cycle. RIDA (Phase 5) drove DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADP, but with no reverse path the ATP form depleted to zero. DARS adds the reverse path: DnaA-ADP → apo-DnaA → DnaA-ATP (the apo-to-ATP step is handled by the still-active MONOMER0-160_RXN equilibrium). At steady state the DARS flux balances the RIDA flux and the ATP fraction stabilizes inside the literature band. The live bigraph (which now includes dars) is rendered once in Phase 5 → Process connections.

Before — + RIDA (no DARS, no binding; mass gate)

Phase 5 alone (RIDA without DARS) drains DnaA-ATP toward zero over the cell cycle because there is no reverse path. The "After" panel shows what DARS does — flux from DnaA-ADP back into apo-DnaA, then ATP-loading via the still-active equilibrium reaction.

After — + RIDA + DARS (no binding; mass gate)

Cycle closed. Final DnaA-ATP fraction 57%. The two fluxes balance at steady state, holding the ATP-fraction inside the band — the biology that Phase 1 was diagnosing as missing.

Caveats & deferred follow-ups
  • DARS1 and DARS2 are not yet differentiated — a single rate constant covers both loci.
  • IHF and Fis modulation of DARS2 (cell-cycle gating) is not yet wired; the rate is constant in time.
  • DARS region coordinates are not loaded into motif_coordinates as their own motif type — the existing EcoCyc dna_sites entries are recognized only via the region classifier from Phase 0.
Test coverage (hover items for descriptions)
  • ✓ tests/test_dars.py4 tests
    test_dars_step_in_cell_state — dars step in cell state
    test_atp_fraction_stabilizes_inside_literature_band — After 5 minutes the cycle has closed: DnaA-ATP fraction sits
    test_dars_listener_emits — dars listener emits
    test_dnaA_adp_does_not_monotonically_grow — Without DARS, DnaA-ADP rises monotonically and DnaA-ATP depletes

Part III — From cytoplasm to chromosome

DnaA binds DNA at DnaA boxes — short consensus sequences clustered at oriC and a handful of regulatory loci, but also scattered across the chromosomal background (~280 sites). The background pool is large enough to titrate the cytoplasmic DnaA-ATP, keeping free concentration low and pacing how fast the named regions can load. Phase 2 samples each box per tick using equilibrium binding (p = [DnaA] / (Kd + [DnaA]), with Kd from the box's affinity class), then allocates the actual DnaA pool to the samples in priority order — highest affinity first — so under contention the named regulatory regions win. Bound molecules are subtracted from bulk[DnaA-ATP] / bulk[DnaA-ADP] so titration is felt by all downstream phases. Critically, oriC has only 3 high-affinity boxes (R1, R2, R4) plus 8 cooperative low-affinity boxes — the low-affinity load is what makes initiation a switch rather than a gradient. Phase 3 replaces the baseline mass-per-oriC threshold with a check on actual oriC occupancy: fire when at least 4 of the 8 cooperative low-affinity oriC boxes are loaded with DnaA-ATP (≈ "right-arm filament assembled", per the PDF). The gate reads bound_oric_low from the Phase 2 binding listener, so initiation is keyed off the actual molecular configuration on the chromosome rather than a cytoplasmic concentration proxy. The cell-cycle self-limiting shape is preserved: after firing, n_oriC doubles, bound_oric_low briefly drops, SeqA (Phase 4) holds the gate shut, and RIDA (Phase 5) drains the DnaA-ATP pool.

Phase 2 — DnaA-box binding + chromosomal titration

done
What this phase does: Add a DnaABoxBinding step that samples per-box equilibrium occupancy each tick from the DnaA-ATP/ADP bulk pools and per-region affinity classes. Emits per-region bound counts (including the ~99 background boxes in `other`, which sequester most of the DnaA pool — the chromosomal titration buffer described in the curated reference). Currently a listener: bound counts are reported but not yet subtracted from the free pool.
Auto-check: DnaABoxBinding step wired into the architecture; per-region bound counts emitted in listeners.dnaA_binding  ·  7 tests

Architecture changes — what this phase adds, modifies, overrides

Why a listener-only process? The unique-array set update mode requires the new value array to match the active-box count exactly. chromosome_structure adds and deletes DnaA_box entries during fork passage in the same tick that binding samples occupancy, so the apply-time count differs from the sample-time count and numpy raises a size mismatch. The listener pattern dodges that conflict — Phase 3 (initiation gating) and the report can both read the listener directly.

KindSummaryFileDetail / wiring
New processDnaABoxBinding Stepv2ecoli/processes/dnaA_box_binding.pyListener-only process: per active DnaA box, samples bound/unbound from the equilibrium occupancy p_bound = [DnaA] / (Kd + [DnaA]). Per-region Kd and nucleotide preference come from the curated reference. Does *not* write back to DnaA_bound on the unique store (the set update mode conflicts with chromosome_structure's add/delete on the same tick); Phase 3 reads the listener counts directly.
reads: bulk[MONOMER0-160[c]], bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], unique.DnaA_box.coordinates, unique.DnaA_box._entryState
writes: listeners.dnaA_binding.total_bound, listeners.dnaA_binding.total_active, listeners.dnaA_binding.fraction_bound, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_<region>
DataREGION_BINDING_RULES + DEFAULT_REGION_BINDING_RULEv2ecoli/data/replication_initiation/molecular_reference.pyPer-region (affinity_class, binds_atp, binds_adp) tuples driving Phase 2's occupancy formula. All named regions currently use the high-affinity class (Kd ~1 nM); the default rule for unnamed regions is low-affinity ATP-preferential (Kd ~100 nM).
ListenerNew listeners.dnaA_binding.* fieldsv2ecoli/processes/dnaA_box_binding.pytotal_bound / total_active / fraction_bound plus bound_oric / bound_dnaA_promoter / bound_datA / bound_DARS1 / bound_DARS2 / bound_other.
writes: listeners.dnaA_binding.total_bound, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_oric, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_dnaA_promoter, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_datA, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_DARS1, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_DARS2, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_other

DnaA-box affinities — per-box detail from the curated reference

Affinity at the DnaA boxes is highly heterogeneous, and at oriC most boxes are low-affinity. The curated reference (Katayama et al. 2017; Kasho, Ozaki, Katayama 2023) reports 3 high-affinity boxes at oriC (R1, R2, R4; Kd ≈ 1 nM, both DnaA-ATP and DnaA-ADP) and 8 low-affinity sites (Kd > 100 nM, DnaA-ATP-preferential, cooperative). Pre-initiation, only the three high-affinity boxes are routinely occupied; the low-affinity DOR boxes fill in cooperatively only after the DnaA-ATP pool is high enough — that's the load-and-trigger mechanism that produces a sharp initiation switch.

Mechanism (from the curated PDF): (1) In the resting cell, R1/R2/R4 hold DnaA in either nucleotide form, and IHF binds IBS1. (2) As the DnaA-ATP pool rises, DnaA-ATP cooperatively assembles on the right arm of the DOR — C1 → I3 → C2 → C3 — anchored by R4. (3) IHF-induced bending promotes a second DnaA-ATP filament on the left arm of the DOR, anchored by R1 with help from R2. (4) Torsional strain from the DnaA oligomers unwinds the adjacent DUE; DnaB helicase loads onto the open single-stranded substrate. The cooperative low-affinity binding is what makes initiation a switch rather than a gradient.

oriC — 11 boxes (3 high-affinity, 8 low-affinity, cooperative DnaA-ATP loading)
BoxClassKdSequence (consensus / variant)Nucleotide form
R1high~1 nMTTATCCACAboth
R2high~1 nMTTATACACAboth
R4high~1 nMTTATCCACAboth
R5Mlow>100 nM—atp
tau2low>100 nM—atp
I1low>100 nM—atp
I2low>100 nM—atp
I3low>100 nM—atp
C1low>100 nM—atp
C2low>100 nM—atp
C3low>100 nM—atp
dnaA promoter — 7 boxes spanning p1 / p2 (~80 bp apart; p2 ≈ 3× stronger than p1)
BoxClassKdSequence (consensus / variant)Nucleotide form
box1high~1 nMTTATCCACAboth
box2high~1 nM—both
box3very_low≫ 100 nM—unspecified
box4low>100 nM—atp
boxalow>100 nM—unspecified
boxblow>100 nM—atp
boxclow>100 nM—atp

How the Phase 2 step uses these: dnaA_box_binding samples each active box per tick against an equilibrium-occupancy probability p = [DnaA] / (Kd + [DnaA]), picking one Kd per region from REGION_BINDING_RULES. This collapses the per-box heterogeneity (and the cooperativity) into a region-level scalar, so it does not yet reproduce the sharp load-and-trigger switch — that's a follow-up. Phase 3 reads the bound counts directly off this listener; the feedback is real even with the simplified affinity model.

Load-and-trigger at oriC — per-tier occupancy over time

The two oriC tiers in action. The 3 high-affinity boxes (green) saturate at their ceiling of 3 within the first tick — at ~100 nM DnaA-ATP and Kd ≈ 1 nM, p_bound = 100 / (1 + 100) ≈ 0.99. The 8 low-affinity boxes (amber) hover well below their ceiling of 8 — at the same concentration but Kd > 100 nM, p_bound ≈ 0.5, so the binomial expectation is ~4. This is the load-and-trigger split that single-Kd sampling collapses.

DnaA-box occupancy view — alternative chromosome diagram

Alternative chromosome view: each region's DnaA boxes are drawn as a fan of dots — filled dots are currently DnaA-bound, hollow dots are unbound. Box totals come from the curated PDF (see PER_REGION_PDF_COUNT); the bound count is sampled each tick by dnaA_box_binding from the DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP pools using per-region affinity rules.

Before — + RIDA + DARS (no binding; mass gate)

After — + RIDA + DARS + binding (mass gate still active)

High-affinity regions (oriC / dnaA promoter / DARS1 / DARS2 — Kd ~ 1 nM) saturate at 100% occupancy. "Other" boxes (the bulk of the strict-consensus motif hits across the genome, falling back to a low-affinity Kd ~ 100 nM rule) sit at ~50–70% occupancy depending on the cellular [DnaA-ATP].

Caveats & deferred follow-ups
  • <strong>Titration is not yet wired into the dynamics.</strong> The listener counts ~250+ bound boxes at steady state but does not decrement the free DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP pool. Phases 3, 5, 7 see the un-buffered total pool, which inflates the available DnaA-ATP for initiation by ~5–10×. The follow-up is to move bound counts into the bulk delta each tick.
  • No write-back to the `DnaA_bound` field on the unique-molecule store — the set-update / add-delete conflict with chromosome_structure is left as a follow-up.
  • All named regions currently use the high-affinity rule. Per-box affinity differentiation (R1/R2/R4 vs named low-affinity oriC sites) and context-dependent binding (variants near high-affinity clusters acting as high-affinity sites via cooperativity) are follow-ups.
Test coverage (hover items for descriptions)
  • ✓ tests/test_dnaA_binding.py7 tests
    test_binding_step_in_cell_state — binding step in cell state
    test_listener_emits_per_region_counts — After the binding step has fired once, the listener exposes
    test_listener_reports_nonzero_bound_count — Phase 2 emits a per-tick equilibrium-occupancy report on the
    test_high_affinity_regions_are_saturated — At ~166 nM [DnaA-ATP] (typical cellular DnaA pool), high-
    test_low_affinity_other_boxes_not_fully_saturated — Boxes outside named regions fall back to low-affinity
    test_per_region_counts_bounded_by_pdf_total — Per-region bound counts are bounded by the curated PDF total
    test_oric_load_and_trigger_split — At any reasonable DnaA-ATP concentration, oriC's high-affinity

Phase 3 — Replace mass-threshold initiation gate

done
What this phase does: Substitute `DnaAGatedChromosomeReplication` for the baseline `ChromosomeReplication`. The new gate fires when at least 4 of the 8 cooperative low-affinity DnaA boxes at oriC are loaded with DnaA-ATP (≈ "right-arm filament assembled" per the curated PDF: R4 → C1 → I3 → C2 → C3). The gate reads `bound_oric_low` from the Phase 2 binding listener, so the trigger is the actual molecular configuration on the chromosome, not a cytoplasmic concentration proxy. Per-tick state-merge ordering means the gate sees `bound_oric_low = 0` at t=0 (before the binding step has sampled), so the cell starts in the cache's correct state of 1 chromosome / 2 oriC / 2 forks rather than firing an extra initiation in tick 0.
Auto-check: DnaAGatedChromosomeReplication subclass swapped in; initiation gates on DnaA-ATP-per-oriC instead of cell-mass-per-oriC  ·  5 tests

Chromosome dynamics — initiation events in space

Each disc shows the chromosome at one snapshot. Green dot = oriC, red square = ter, amber triangles outside the circle = active replisomes (forks). The named regulatory regions are marked around the rim. The bottom strip plots oriC + replisome counts vs time so you can see when initiations fired (red dashed lines mark step-ups in oriC count).

Before — + RIDA + DARS + binding (mass gate still active)

Observed 1 initiation event(s) over 42 min of sim. Each red dashed line marks one (oriC count steps up). The gate ratio in the lower panel crosses 1.0 at each event and drops back below afterward — the per-oriC denominator provides the self-limiting feedback that both gates share.

After — DnaA-gated initiation (no SeqA sequestration)

Observed 3 initiation event(s) over 60 min of sim. Each red dashed line marks one (oriC count steps up). The gate ratio in the lower panel crosses 1.0 at each event and drops back below afterward — the per-oriC denominator provides the self-limiting feedback that both gates share.

Caveats & deferred follow-ups
  • Threshold of 4 of 8 low-affinity boxes is a first-pass tune for the right-arm filament. The PDF's full load-and-trigger criterion would also require the left-arm filament (R1-anchored) to be loaded; our binding listener doesn't resolve right-arm vs left-arm boxes — it reports them as a single low-affinity tier.
  • No cooperativity in the binding step: each low-affinity box is sampled independently every tick, so the tier fills in one tick once DnaA-ATP rises high enough. A faithful model would require ordered, anchor-conditional loading (C1 → I3 → C2 → C3) over a finite kinetic time.
  • No DnaB helicase loading or DUE-opening kinetics — the transition from "gate fired" to "two replisomes moving" is treated as instantaneous.
Test coverage (hover items for descriptions)
  • ✓ tests/test_initiation_dnaA_gate.py5 tests
    test_dnaA_gated_subclass_is_in_cell_state — The architecture replaces the baseline ChromosomeReplication
    test_critical_mass_per_oric_listener_is_dnaA_ratio — The base class's `critical_mass_per_oriC` listener field still
    test_initiation_fires_at_least_once — Over a 30-minute window the cell should fire at least one
    test_initiation_self_limits — After firing once, the DnaA-ATP-per-oriC value should drop
    test_disabling_gate_falls_back_to_baseline_class — ``enable_dnaA_gated_initiation=False`` skips the swap; the

Part IV — Regulatory feedbacks

Three timing-control layers sit on top of the DnaA cycle. Phase 4 (SeqA) sequesters the newly-replicated origin for ~10 minutes after each firing — modeling SeqA binding to hemimethylated GATC sites at oriC and physically blocking DnaA rebinding, so the cell cannot fire again immediately. Phase 6 (DDAH) adds a constitutive backup DnaA-ATP hydrolysis term that represents the IHF-induced loop at the datA locus: less powerful than RIDA but always on, so DnaA-ATP cannot drift upward between replication rounds. Phase 8 (dnaA promoter autoregulation) closes the loop on the whole cycle: when DnaA binds the seven boxes at its own p1 / p2 promoters, transcription of dnaA is repressed, scaling the gene's baseline transcription rate in the live transcript-initiation step.

Phase 4 — SeqA sequestration window after initiation

done
What this phase does: SeqA the protein (`EG12197-MONOMER`) is already expressed (~1029 copies in the init state); Phase 4 wires its *activity*. The DnaA-gated step records the time of each initiation event and forces its gate ratio to 0 for the next ~10 minutes — modeling SeqA binding to hemimethylated GATC sites at the newly-replicated origin and blocking DnaA rebinding during the window.
Auto-check: SeqA refractory window wired into the DnaA-gated chromosome-replication step (default 600s)  ·  5 tests

Architecture changes — what this phase adds, modifies, overrides

Why the refractory window approach? Modeling hemimethylated-GATC tracking + SeqA-multimer binding on the unique-molecule store would conflict with chromosome_structure's in-tick add/delete (same set-update issue we hit in Phase 2). The refractory window captures the biology that matters most for cell-cycle timing — SeqA blocks rebinding for a fixed window after each initiation — without requiring a hemimethylation field on the oriC unique molecule. Future refinement: read the bulk SeqA count and decrement / reset the window if SeqA is depleted.

KindSummaryFileDetail / wiring
OverrideSeqA refractory window in DnaAGatedChromosomeReplicationv2ecoli/processes/chromosome_replication_dnaA_gated.pyThe DnaA-gated subclass tracks the previous tick's oriC count; when it jumps, an initiation just fired and global_time is recorded. While (global_time - last_init) < seqA_sequestration_window_s (default 600s), `_compute_dnaA_gate` returns 0 — the gate is shut. Models SeqA binding to hemimethylated GATC sites at the newly-replicated origin.
reads: states.oriCs._entryState, states.global_time
writes: self._previous_n_oric, self._last_initiation_time_s
DataSeqA already in bulk; no protein addition neededv2ecoli/processes/parca/reconstruction/ecoli/flat/proteins.tsvSeqA monomer (`EG12197-MONOMER`) is already expressed by the baseline transcription/translation pipeline at ~1029 copies in the init state. Phase 4 wires its *activity* downstream of the gate — a future refinement would consume SeqA stoichiometrically (one bound multimer per sequestered origin) so SeqA scarcity could shorten the window.
Overrideenable_seqA_sequestration flag (default True)v2ecoli/generate_replication_initiation.pybuild_replication_initiation_document gains `enable_seqA_sequestration` (default True). When True, `_swap_in_dnaA_gated_chromosome_replication` instantiates the subclass with `seqA_sequestration_window_s=600.0`. When False, window=0 — gate has no refractory.

Before — DnaA-gated initiation (no SeqA sequestration)

Without SeqA, the gate signal is just the DnaA-ATP-per-oriC ratio at every tick. Reinitiation timing is set entirely by the DnaA pool dynamics — there is no refractory window after a firing event.

After — gate + SeqA, no DDAH backup hydrolysis

After the initiation event, the gate is forced to 0 for the SeqA sequestration window (~10 min, shaded amber). Once the window expires, the gate resumes reading the actual DnaA-ATP-per-oriC ratio — and because RIDA+DARS dynamics keep the per-oriC value below 1.0, no immediate re-initiation fires.

Caveats & deferred follow-ups
  • No hemimethylated-GATC tracking on the oriC unique molecule — the refractory window is a coarser proxy for the SeqA-binding biology. Adding a hemimethylation field would conflict with chromosome_structure's in-tick add/delete (same set-update issue as Phase 2).
  • No stoichiometric coupling to bulk SeqA. The window is a fixed-duration cap; depleted SeqA does not shorten it. A future refinement would scale the window by the bulk SeqA count.
Test coverage (hover items for descriptions)
  • ✓ tests/test_seqA_sequestration.py5 tests
    test_seqA_protein_already_expressed — Sanity check — the SeqA protein is already in the bulk pool
    test_sequestration_window_set_when_flag_on — sequestration window set when flag on
    test_gate_closed_during_sequestration_window — After the t=0 initiation event, the gate ratio should be 0
    test_gate_resumes_after_window_expires — At t=900s (300s past the 600s window), the gate ratio must
    test_disabling_seqA_keeps_gate_open — ``enable_seqA_sequestration=False`` skips the sequestration

Phase 6 — DDAH — backup DnaA-ATP hydrolysis at datA

done
What this phase does: Add a DDAH Step that hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP at a small constitutive rate, modeling the catalytic effect of the datA-IHF complex described in the curated reference. Pairs with Phase 5 (RIDA): RIDA dominates while replisomes are active, DDAH provides a steady background drain that runs even between replication rounds.
Auto-check: DDAH process spliced into the architecture; constitutive first-order DnaA-ATP hydrolysis runs alongside RIDA  ·  4 tests

Architecture changes — what this phase adds, modifies, overrides

What's deferred for the first cut: DDAH currently fires constitutively — it does not gate on IHF binding at datA, and the bioinformatic strict-consensus search finds 0 boxes in the datA window so per-box occupancy is not used. Adding datA region coordinates to motif_coordinates and gating on the IHF heterodimer count are the natural follow-ups.

KindSummaryFileDetail / wiring
New processDDAH Stepv2ecoli/processes/ddah.pyA constitutive first-order DnaA-ATP -> DnaA-ADP hydrolyzer modeling the catalytic effect of the datA-IHF complex. Rate = k_ddah * dnaA_atp_count, Poisson-drawn each tick. Default rate is ~10x slower than RIDA per replisome so DDAH stays a backup.
reads: bulk[MONOMER0-160[c]], bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], timestep
writes: bulk[MONOMER0-160[c]], bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], listeners.ddah.flux_atp_to_adp, listeners.ddah.rate_constant
ListenerNew listeners.ddah.* fieldsv2ecoli/processes/ddah.pyPer-tick DDAH hydrolysis flux + configured rate constant.
writes: listeners.ddah.flux_atp_to_adp, listeners.ddah.rate_constant
Overrideenable_ddah flag (default True)v2ecoli/generate_replication_initiation.pybuild_replication_initiation_document gains `enable_ddah` (default True). When True, `_splice_ddah` adds the step to flow_order alongside RIDA / DARS / box binding.

Before — gate + SeqA, no DDAH backup hydrolysis

Without DDAH, RIDA is the only DnaA-ATP hydrolyzer. The ATP fraction sits closer to the upper edge of the literature band because nothing pulls it down between replication pulses.

After — replication_initiation (gate + SeqA + DDAH)

Over this trajectory, RIDA contributed 49 hydrolysis events vs DDAH's 0. RIDA dominates while replisomes are active; DDAH provides a steady background drain that runs even between replication rounds.

Caveats & deferred follow-ups
  • datA region coordinates not yet in `motif_coordinates` — the strict-consensus motif search finds 0 boxes in the datA window. Per-box occupancy is therefore not used; DDAH fires at a constant rate independent of datA box count.
  • IHF heterodimer count is not yet read by DDAH. The biology gates DDAH on IHF binding at the datA IBS; we approximate with a constant rate instead.
  • Rate constant (`rate_per_s = 0.0005`) is a first-pass tune. With it, the ATP fraction can dip slightly below the lower edge of the literature band — would need recalibration once the IHF gating is wired.
Test coverage (hover items for descriptions)
  • ✓ tests/test_ddah.py4 tests
    test_ddah_step_in_cell_state — ddah step in cell state
    test_ddah_listener_emits_rate_constant — ddah listener emits rate constant
    test_ddah_produces_nonzero_cumulative_flux — Over ~5 minutes, DDAH should have hydrolyzed at least a few
    test_disabling_ddah_removes_step — ``enable_ddah=False`` skips the splice.

Phase 8 — dnaA promoter autoregulation — close the loop on the DnaA cycle

done
What this phase does: Add a dedicated `DnaAAutoregulation` Step that reads the binding listener's `bound_dnaA_promoter` count each tick and rescales the dnaA TU's `basal_prob` in the live `TranscriptInitiation` instance. The repression factor is `1 - max_repression × bound/total`, identity at zero occupancy and minimum `1 - max_repression` at saturation. The Parca-fit baseline is captured at attach-time and re-applied each tick, so the feedback is fully reversible. p1 vs p2 splitting and the DnaA-ADP boost reported at p2 are deferred follow-ups.
Auto-check: DnaAAutoregulation step rescales dnaA TU basal_prob by bound_dnaA_promoter occupancy each tick  ·  4 tests

Before — pre_autoreg

Pre-Phase-8 cumulative slice: the full architecture is in place, except dnaA_autoregulation is disabled. Occupancy at the dnaA promoter (top) varies with the DnaA pool, but the dnaA transcription rate (bottom) is pinned to its Parca-fit baseline — there is nothing reading the occupancy.

After — replication_initiation (gate + SeqA + DDAH)

Phase 8 closes the loop. The repression factor 1 - max_repression × bound/total rescales the dnaA TU's basal_prob in the live TranscriptInitiation step each tick. With max_repression = 0.7, full saturation drives ~3-fold repression. The applied basal_prob (lower panel, purple) drops in concert with the bound count (upper panel, blue), exactly anti-correlated with the autoregulator's driver.

Caveats & deferred follow-ups
  • No p1 / p2 promoter splitting yet — both share the same TU-level basal_prob and the same scaling factor.
  • The default `max_repression = 0.7` (~3-fold repression at saturation) is a first-pass tune; not calibrated to measured fold-repression numbers.
  • Recent reports of *positive* regulation at p2 are not modeled.
Test coverage (hover items for descriptions)
  • ✓ tests/test_dnaA_autoregulation.py4 tests
    test_autoregulation_step_in_cell_state — autoregulation step in cell state
    test_autoregulation_listener_emits_factor — autoregulation listener emits factor
    test_disabling_autoregulation_removes_step — ``enable_dnaA_autoregulation=False`` skips the splice.
    test_basal_prob_scaled_by_occupancy — The Step rescales the dnaA TU's basal_prob in proportion to

Mechanisms in the curated PDF that are not yet modeled

The migration as it stands turns the mass-per-oriC heuristic into a DnaA-centered regulatory network — but several mechanisms named in the curated reference are still represented as simplifications. Each one is a candidate follow-up; the model would gain biological fidelity from any of them.

Per-box affinity / cooperative DnaA filament loading
The Phase 2 binding step now distinguishes high-affinity from low-affinity boxes per region (so oriC's 8 low-affinity sites fill on a different curve than the 3 high-affinity ones), but the curated PDF describes the low-affinity load as cooperative and ordered: along the right arm of the DOR the order is C1 → I3 → C2 → C3, anchored by R4-bound DnaA, with a second filament on the left arm anchored by R1. The current model treats the low-affinity boxes as independent — no cooperativity coefficient, no anchor dependency. Adding a Hill term or explicit filament-loading kinetics would reproduce the load-and-trigger switch.
Instantaneous equilibrium binding / no loading kinetics
The Phase 2 binding step samples each oriC box independently from an equilibrium occupancy probability every tick. So when DnaA-ATP rises, the low-affinity boxes fill in a single tick rather than over a few seconds of cooperative loading. This lets the Phase 3 gate fire immediately once bound_oric_low crosses threshold, rather than requiring the load-and-trigger switch to actually accumulate. A faithful model would track loading kinetics — DnaA-ATP arriving at the open boxes at some on-rate, with a refractory window after R4 / R1 anchor before the cooperative quartet can populate.
IHF binding and its locus-specific roles
IHF (Integration Host Factor) is a nucleoid-associated DNA-bending protein. The PDF assigns it three distinct regulatory roles: at oriC it bends DNA between R1 and R5M (IBS1) to promote DnaA-ATP filament formation on the left arm; at datA it induces a loop that catalyzes DnaA-ATP hydrolysis (Phase 6 / DDAH); at DARS2 it gates the locus cell-cycle timing. None of these IHF-driven structural events are wired today — DDAH runs at a constant rate, oriC filament formation is implicit, DARS2 timing is approximated as constant.
Fis modulation of DARS2
The DARS2 locus is gated in vivo by a balance of IHF and Fis (Factor for Inversion Stimulation) binding. Fis is already expressed in v2ecoli but its DARS2 binding sites (FBS1, FBS2-3) are not wired — so DARS1 and DARS2 currently share a single first-order rate, with no cell-cycle dependence.
Hda nucleotide-state requirement for RIDA
Per the curated PDF, RIDA is catalyzed by an ADP-loaded Hda (specifically Hda-ADP), interacting with the moving β-clamp via Hda's N-terminal clamp-binding motif. The Phase 5 RIDA step assumes Hda-ADP is always available — it reads the active replisome count directly. A faithful model would track the Hda nucleotide-state pool and gate the RIDA rate on Hda-ADP availability.
SeqA at the dnaA promoter
The dnaA-promoter region is GATC-rich, so SeqA also binds there (not just at oriC) for the brief window after the replisome passes through it. This buffers the transient gene-dosage spike and contributes to dnaA expression dynamics in the cell cycle. The Phase 4 SeqA sequestration window currently only blocks initiation; it does not modulate transcription at the dnaA promoter.
p1 vs p2 differential regulation at the dnaA promoter
The dnaA promoter has two start sites: p1 at basal level and p2 ≈ 3× stronger; recent work reports that DnaA can act as both a positive and a negative regulator at p2 specifically. Phase 8 collapses both promoters into a single TU-level basal_prob and applies a single repression scale — preserving the loop topology but losing the p1 / p2 asymmetry.
DnaB helicase loading and DUE opening dynamics
Once enough DnaA-ATP is loaded onto the DOR, the resulting DnaA filament unwinds the DUE and DnaB helicase loads onto the exposed single-stranded DNA — that's what physically converts "gate fired" into "two replisomes moving outward". The current model treats this transition as instantaneous: one tick after the gate fires, the replisomes appear in the unique-store. The actual delay is short (seconds) but a more detailed model would track the DnaB loading reaction.
Position-specific motif preferences
The PDF reports preferences within the consensus 9-mer: position 3 prefers A > T, position 5 prefers C > A ≥ G > T. The current binding step ignores per-position effects and treats every consensus match the same. A more refined model would weight box affinity by motif-position scores.
Total DnaA pool size vs. titration demand
Phase 2 now applies titration: bound DnaA molecules are decremented from the cytoplasmic bulk[DnaA-ATP] / bulk[DnaA-ADP] pools. With ~500 active boxes across the chromosome and the cache's ~120-molecule DnaA pool, the free pool is essentially zeroed out within a few ticks. Priority allocation (highest affinity first) gets the named regulatory regions saturated, but the background "other" pool is starved for DnaA. The literature DnaA pool is closer to 1000–2000 molecules per cell — much larger than the cache's apo-DnaA count. Bumping the DnaA expression rate (or pre-loading more apo-DnaA in the cache) would let the background actually pull a meaningful fraction.
Per-region competitive binding within an affinity tier
Under contention, the priority allocation iterates regions in tier order but doesn't differentiate within a tier — e.g., all five "high" tiers (one per named region) are equal. A more faithful model would weight by absolute Kd or by binding kinetics (which would favor oriC's R1/R2/R4 over the dnaA promoter's box1/box2 if their Kds differ).

oriC

462 bp, 11 DnaA boxes, 2 IHF sites. High-affinity boxes bind both DnaA-ATP and DnaA-ADP (Kd ~1 nM). Low-affinity boxes prefer DnaA-ATP and bind cooperatively (Kd > 100 nM). Ordered DnaA-ATP loading on the right arm: C1 → I3 → C2 → C3.

BoxClassSequenceNucleotide preference
R1highTTATCCACAboth
R2highTTATACACAboth
R4highTTATCCACAboth
R5Mlow—atp
tau2low—atp
I1low—atp
I2low—atp
I3low—atp
C1low—atp
C2low—atp
C3low—atp

dnaA promoter

448 bp; p2 ≈ 3× p1; ~80 bp separation.

BoxClassSequenceNucleotide preference
box1highTTATCCACAboth
box2high—both
box3very_low—unspecified
box4low—atp
boxalow—unspecified
boxblow—atp
boxclow—atp

datA

363 bp at 94.7 min  |  DnaA boxes: 4  |  essential: box2, box3, box7  |  stimulatory: box4  |  IHF sites: 1

DARS1 / DARS2

LocusbpCore boxesExtra boxesIHF sitesFis sitesDominant
DARS1632I, II, III—00no
DARS2737I, II, IIIIV, V12yes

SeqA / RIDA

SeqA sequestration window: 10 min (~0.33 of doubling time at rapid growth). SeqA binds hemimethylated GATC sites (>10 per origin).

RIDA: DnaN (β-clamp) + ADP-Hda (N-terminal clamp-binding motif) catalyzes DnaA-ATP -> DnaA-ADP.

Consensus motifs

Consensus: TTWTNCACA (W = A|T, N = any). Highest-affinity 9-mer: TTATCCACA (Kd ~1 nM).

Trajectory under the new architecture

Ran (or reused cached) single-cell sims for three cumulative configurations: baseline → +RIDA → +DARS (full). Each up to 3600s.

Key references

  • Katayama, Kasho, Kawakami. Frontiers in Microbiology 8 (2017): 2496.
  • Kasho, Ozaki, Katayama. Int. J. Mol. Sci. 24 (2023): 11572.
  • Speck, Weigel, Messer. EMBO J. 18 (1999): 6169–6176.
  • Schaper & Messer. J. Biol. Chem. 270 (1995): 17622–17626.
  • Hansen et al. J. Mol. Biol. 355 (2006): 85–95.
  • Olivi et al. Nat. Commun. 16 (2025): 7813.
  • Riber et al. Front. Mol. Biosci. 3 (2016): 29.
  • Hansen & Atlung. Front. Microbiol. 9 (2018): 319.
  • Fujimitsu, Senriuchi, Katayama. Genes & Dev. 23 (2009): 1221–1233.
  • Kasho et al. Nucleic Acids Res. 42 (2014): 13134–13149.
